assumptions: A supported workstation, terminal, editor, and outbound package-download access are available.
No Temporal account, Cloud Namespace, SDK, CLI, local service, Workflow, Activity, Worker, or language selection is assumed.
The local Hello World quickstarts do not require a Temporal Cloud account.

developer_goal: Use Temporal's official Quickstart to set up a local development service and run the documented Hello World Workflow.

first_success.outcome: In the Go candidate, the local Worker processes the Workflow and Activity, the starter prints `Workflow result: Hello Temporal`, and the execution is visible in the local Web UI.
first_success.observable_signal: The terminal prints `Workflow result: Hello Temporal`, while Worker processing and Workflow details are observable in the Worker terminal and Web UI.

route_selection.surface: Official Temporal Quickstarts (local SDK Hello World)
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Go SDK on macOS with local Hello World
candidates.reason: A reconstructable local candidate, but Temporal lists eight peer SDK languages without declaring a universal default.

candidates.name: Java Hello World Quickstart
candidates.reason: Java is a peer SDK with different runtime, build, Worker, and starter commands.

candidates.name: Python Hello World Quickstart
candidates.reason: Python is a peer SDK and Temporal does not rank it below or above Go.

candidates.name: TypeScript, PHP, Ruby, .NET, or Rust Hello World Quickstart
candidates.reason: Additional peer language quickstarts on the same Quickstarts index.

candidates.name: Temporal Cloud
candidates.reason: The Quickstarts index explicitly separates local language quickstarts from the account, Namespace, and authentication path for Temporal Cloud.

required_path.action: Obtain and install the prerequisites required by the selected Temporal candidate route.

required_fields.label: Install or download choices
required_fields.notes: Exact installer, OS, version, or package selectors are release-dependent or peer-branched.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a runnable environment for the selected candidate.

required_path.action: Follow the documented commands for the selected Temporal first-success candidate through its terminal verification.
required_path.observable_result: The terminal prints `Workflow result: Hello Temporal`, while Worker processing and Workflow details are observable in the Worker terminal and Web UI.

prerequisites.description: Choose one supported Temporal SDK language; the Quickstarts index does not select a universal language.
For the Go candidate, install Go and confirm it with `go version`; the tutorial was produced with Go 1.18.
Install Temporal CLI for the workstation operating system; the Go local guide documents Homebrew on macOS and alternate Windows and Linux downloads.

unavoidable_waits.description: Package download, image pull, or local Temporal Service startup time for the selected install route.

platform_outcomes.description: The local Temporal Service exposes the default Namespace, gRPC endpoint, and Web UI while it remains running.
A successful Hello World run prints the Workflow result and records the execution in the local Web UI.

excluded.item: Temporal Cloud account, Namespace, and authentication
excluded.reason: The Quickstarts index separates Cloud onboarding from local language Hello World routes.

excluded.item: Building a second application, courses, Signals or Queries, failure-recovery drills, production Worker deploy, or self-hosting
excluded.reason: Listed after first success in the local quickstart material and outside the Hello World terminal.

excluded.item: Reading Temporal documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Temporal Docs | Temporal Platform Documentation
evidence_supported: Primary documentation entry and first-success framing

sources.title: Set up your local with the Go SDK | Temporal Platform Documentation

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for Temporal's local Hello World path, which requires no Temporal Cloud account?
uncertainties.impact: The schema forces starting_state.boundary to account creation, but the selected local quickstarts begin at SDK and CLI install. Counts cannot be published as verified under the account-creation contract without an explicit OSS or no-account exception.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy decision that either (a) treats no-account local SDK quickstarts as an explicit exception with a different starting boundary, or (b) requires selecting Temporal Cloud as the measured route.

uncertainties.question: Which Temporal SDK language and workstation OS route should define the platform-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes prerequisites, package installation, application files, commands, and terminal presentation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded goal naming one language and OS, after confirming the Quickstarts index still presents peer languages without a universal default.
